incorrects.description: Student selected Odd: believes multiplying by 2 produces odd products

steps.text: Look at what happens. When you add 2 plus 2, you get 4. Add another 2, you get 6. Every time you add 2 to an even number, you stay even. Are any of these totals odd?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Visual emphasis appears under the product 10 in 5 × 2 = 10.

steps.text: Let me show you. 5 times 2 equals 10. 10 is even. 3 times 2 equals 6. 6 is even. 7 times 2 equals 14. 14 is even. Every product of 2 is even because multiplying by 2 means making groups of 2, and groups of 2 always pair up perfectly with no items left over. The answer is always even.
